opp.name: Estremar_P2P+SIM_Apr'26
opp.next_step: 6/29 BCE: Hasnt viewed demos, but acknowledged them. 5/30 BCE: They are viewing videos and will get back in the next week 5/20 BCE: Sent follow up email 5/8 BCE: Demo videos sent 4/24 BCE: Sending demo videos to team
opp.ais_why:
• Both Estremar meetings were either cancelled or had no recording, so there is no spoken AI signal to anchor on.
• SF Next_Step shows only 'demo videos sent' for a P2P+SIM scope with no Merlin, ANA, intake or agentic mentions in the opp description.
• Sparse data, no transcripts, and a tactical P2P/SIM frame point to Resistant by default.

sources: Email 31 March 2026, Gianluca Pansera: manual invoice loading prevents analysis and KPI generation (source: Task description)
Email 31 March 2026, Pansera: new CFO mandate to automate AP analyst role, preparing for capital markets (source: Task description)
Email 31 March 2026, Pansera: operational need for supplier portal with invoice approval workflow, ERP integration, and automated accounting entries (source: Task description)
metrics.narrative: Pansera articulated a clear operational pain in the 31 March 2026 email: manual invoice entry into the ERP prevents him from doing analysis and generating KPIs for his role. He stated the new CFO's mandate is to automate the Accounts Payable analyst position through a supplier portal. However, no financial metrics were quantified (cost of manual processing, cycle time, error rate, headcount savings, or ROI). The pain is confirmed qualitative; the business case remains unquantified. The email thread shows the business problem (manual AP workload, preparing for capital markets requiring tighter controls), but without a dollar or efficiency figure we cannot anchor to a defendable ROI. Estremar is a Norwegian-owned fishing company (black hake export) preparing for an IPO, which implies urgency and executive scrutiny, but that compelling event has not been tied to a timeline or a quantified procurement automation target.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: Gianluca Pansera, Analyst of Accounts for Pay, Evaluator (source: SOQL)
Email thread March-May 2026: Pansera responsive, detailed requirements, confirmed CFO budget approval (source: Task descriptions)
Next_Step 28 May 2026 and 29 June 2026: Pansera acknowledged demo videos but has not viewed them 51 days later (source: Next_Step__c, Next_Step_History__c)
No evidence of Pansera introducing Zycus to CFO, procurement lead, or any other stakeholder (source: Task/Event log, contact roles)
champion.narrative: Gianluca Pansera, Analyst of Accounts for Pay, is the only identified contact. He is engaged and responsive in the email thread (asked for demos, described requirements, confirmed 3-way matching need, said CFO approved budget), but he is an analyst with no budget authority, no ability to drive executive engagement, and no evidence of active advocacy. A champion must have access to the economic buyer or decision maker and must coach us on how to win; Pansera has shown neither. He acknowledged demo videos on 8 May but has not viewed them 51 days later despite saying on 28 May he would review them in the second week of June. This is passive, not champion behavior. He is a friendly evaluator who can articulate requirements, but he cannot move the deal without executive engagement, and he has not facilitated that. Champion strength is weak. We are single-threaded at the wrong level.

sources: Competitors__c, Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c all null (source: Q3 SOQL)
No competitor mention in Next_Step__c, Next_Step_History__c, Task descriptions, or Event descriptions (source: SOQL)
Email 1 April 2026, Alexa Bradley: stated $80k-$120k annual price range; Pansera confirmed CFO ok, but no mention of other vendors being evaluated (source: Task description)
competition.narrative: Competitive landscape is completely unknown. No competitor has been named in any Salesforce field, email, task, or call. We do not know if Estremar is sole-sourcing Zycus, running a multi-vendor RFP, or talking to other P2P and supplier portal vendors (Coupa, SAP Ariba, Ivalua, Jaggaer, GEP, Tradeshift). We do not know if they have an incumbent AP or procurement system being replaced or if this is a greenfield build. Alexa Bradley's 1 April email stated the $80k-$120k price range; Pansera said the CFO approved it, but we have no visibility into whether that budget is exclusive to Zycus or shared across a competitive field. Without competitive intelligence we cannot position differentiation, cannot counter a rival's narrative, and cannot know if we are the frontrunner or one of many. This is a critical gap for a deal 82 days from close.

sources: Email 31 March 2026, Pansera: 'Hoy en día, estoy cargando facturas de forma manual en nuestro ERP, lo que me dificulta tener tiempo para hacer análisis y generar indicadores propios del puesto' (source: Task description)
Email 31 March 2026, Pansera: new CFO mandate to implement supplier portal and automate AP analyst position (source: Task description)
Email 31 March 2026, Pansera: preparing for capital markets (IPO), new directors driving operational change (source: Task description)
Customer_Business_Problem__c, Compelling_Event__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c all null in Salesforce fields, but pain is documented in email thread (source: Q3 SOQL vs Task descriptions)
identify_pain.narrative: Gianluca Pansera, Analyst of Accounts for Pay, is the pain owner. He stated on 31 March 2026 that he manually loads invoices into the ERP today, which prevents him from doing analysis and generating KPIs. The new CFO initiated the supplier portal project to automate Pansera's role as part of broader operational changes ahead of Estremar's planned capital markets entry (IPO preparation). The pain is operational (manual, repetitive AP workload) and political (new leadership mandate for automation and reporting rigor). The compelling event is the IPO preparation, which brings new directors, tighter controls, and executive scrutiny. However, no timeline for the IPO or a drop-dead date for the portal go-live has been stated, so urgency is implied but not quantified. The pain is real and confirmed; the forcing function is soft.

sources: Email 31 March 2026, Pansera: ERP integration required (invoices sync to ERP, generate journal entries, update AP and VAT books) (source: Task description)
No tasks, events, or Next_Step entries referencing MSA, legal, InfoSec, SOW, or implementation scoping (source: Task/Event SOQL, Next_Step__c)
Current_Contract_Expiration__c null (source: Q2 SOQL)
paper_process.narrative: Contracting, legal, InfoSec, and implementation mechanics are completely unknown. No discussion of MSA, SOW, order form, redlining, InfoSec review, or ERP integration scoping has occurred. Pansera's emails describe the desired ERP integration (invoices must sync back and generate accounting entries), which implies an integration and implementation project, but no technical scoping call, implementation timeline, or services estimate has been documented. There is no mention of whether Estremar has standard procurement T&Cs, legal review requirements, or data residency constraints (Argentina, Norwegian parent). The deal is pre-commercial; paper process visibility will require a commercial conversation that has not yet happened.

sources: Email 23 April 2026, Pansera: 'hablé con el CFO y me dijo que estaba ok' re pricing (source: Task description)
Account contact Diego Pace, CFO, email diego.pace@estremar.com, but zero opportunity contact role or task/event engagement (source: prefetch contact list)
OpportunityContactRole query returned one contact: Gianluca Pansera, Analyst, Evaluator role (source: SOQL)
Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c: false (source: Q3 SOQL)
economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is unidentified and has never engaged. Pansera mentioned on 23 April 2026 that he spoke to the CFO (the CFO approved the $80k-$120k annual budget range Alexa Bradley proposed on 1 April), but the CFO has never been named, added as a contact role, or joined a call. The account-level contact list shows Diego Pace as CFO, but he has no opportunity contact role and no logged engagement with this deal. Budget authority for a $100k SaaS deal at a 100-person fishing company preparing for IPO unequivocally sits with the CFO or VP Finance, not an AP analyst. Pansera's 23 April statement that the CFO said the pricing was ok is second-hand; we have no direct confirmation, no commercial conversation on record, and no executive sponsor. This is a critical gap: without EB identification and engagement, the deal cannot progress past Qualified, and the forecast is indefensible.

sources: Next_Step_History__c and email tasks show only Pansera correspondence; no other buyer participants (source: SOQL Next_Step_History__c, Task log)
What_is_the_decision_process__c, Mandate__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Multiple_approvals__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c all null (source: Q3 SOQL)
Two Avoma meetings (12 March cancelled, 23 March completed but not recorded); no live discovery call to map process (source: Avoma manifest)
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is unknown. Pansera is the sole identified stakeholder; no multi-person evaluation committee, no calls with procurement or finance leadership, and no mention of an RFP, vendor selection timeline, or approval chain. The email thread shows Pansera asked for demo videos, said he would review them, but then did not (51 days later they remain unviewed). There is no evidence of a structured evaluation, no gating milestones, and no signal that Estremar is running a competitive process or has a vendor down-select planned. We do not know if the CFO must approve, if legal or IT security review is required, if an implementation committee exists, or what the path from demo video to contract looks like. The deal is stuck at the first gate (Pansera reviewing content) with no visibility into what comes after.

sources: Email 24 April 2026, Pansera: 5-point supplier portal functional spec (PO routing, invoice upload, approval, ERP sync with accounting, payment notification) (source: Task description)
Email 30 April 2026, Pansera: 3-way matching required; PO approval workflow tied to account codes; goods receipt gates invoice entry (source: Task description)
Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c all null (source: Q3 SOQL)
decision_criteria.narrative: Pansera detailed functional requirements in his 24 April and 31 March 2026 emails: supplier portal must route POs from ERP to supplier, allow supplier to upload invoices, trigger approval workflow, sync approved invoices back to ERP with automatic accounting entries (journal, AP ledger, VAT book), enable general supplier communication, and send payment confirmations. He also confirmed 30 April that 3-way matching (PO, receipt, invoice) is required, and that PO approval in the ERP is tied to account codes and happens before goods receipt. These are detailed functional criteria. However, they are technical/workflow requirements from one analyst, not a buying-committee-agreed evaluation scorecard. We do not know the CFO's or procurement lead's criteria, whether Estremar is running an RFP or multi-vendor bake-off, what the decision scorecard weighs (cost vs speed vs ERP fit vs localization), or how Zycus will be scored. The criteria Pansera named are necessary but not the full decision picture.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 3.0 Negative (AI Resistant). Both Avoma meetings were cancelled or not recorded, so no spoken AI signal. Salesforce Next_Step shows only P2P and SIM scope with no Merlin, ANA, intake, or agentic mentions. Email thread is tactical supplier portal and invoice automation, no AI appetite expressed. However, the context (IPO prep, new CFO driving analytics and KPIs, manual AP workload) suggests latent fit for Merlin analytics and ANA if positioned as the intelligence layer for capital markets readiness. Buyer has not asked for AI, but the pain (lack of analysis, no KPIs) and the compelling event (IPO scrutiny) could support an AI Curious re-rating if the narrative is activated.

items.change: Demo videos sent to Gianluca Pansera and team
Pansera replied: on vacation, will view videos second week of June (after month-end close)
Close date slipped from 2026-07-12 to 2026-09-17 (67-day slip)
Next_Step updated: Pansera has not viewed demos but acknowledged them
Division__c changed from New Markets to Mid-Enterprise
Rep outreach: Pansera still has not viewed demos but acknowledged them

deal_movement.summary: Deal created 24 April 2026 and moved to Qualified same day. Close date slipped 67 days (from 12 July to 17 September) on 30 May. Last buyer engagement 28 May when Pansera said he would view demo videos in second week of June; that milestone passed with no follow-up. Rep has updated Next_Step six times since 8 May, all noting videos sent, Pansera acknowledged but not viewed. No stage progression, no new stakeholder engagement, no commercial conversation in 64 days. Single action stalled 51 days.

items.name: Diego Pace
items.risk: Unengaged. Budget authority confirmed second-hand 64 days ago; no contact role, no call, no follow-up. Must be identified and engaged to progress past Qualified.
items.title: Chief Financial Officer
items.sentiment: Unknown: approved $80k-$120k pricing per Pansera on 23 April, but no direct engagement with Zycus.

items.name: Gianluca Pansera
items.risk: Passive engagement; may be gathering info without decision authority or urgency. No upward access to CFO or procurement lead.
items.title: Analyst of Accounts for Pay
items.sentiment: Neutral to positive: responsive in email, detailed requirements, but has not viewed demo videos 51 days after delivery despite two commitments.

items.detail: No economic buyer or executive sponsor identified; deal is single-threaded at analyst level (Gianluca Pansera, Evaluator) with no upward access to the CFO or finance VP who controls budget and commercial decisions for a $100k SaaS deal. Without executive sponsorship, the deal is at high risk of stalling or losing to a competitor with stronger C-level alignment.

Close date 17 September (83 days out) is not credible for a Qualified-stage deal with no live buyer engagement in 29 days, no champion, no pain articulation, no decision criteria, no commercial discussions, and no forward milestone booked. The deal has been in Qualified stage for 63 days with no progression. To close 17 September requires moving through Formal Evaluation, Shortlisted, Vendor Selected, Negotiation, and Contract stages in 83 days (average 16 days per stage), yet the deal has shown zero velocity and the buyer has deprioritized (demo videos unviewed 48 days after delivery).

Buyer has not viewed demo videos 48 days after delivery (sent 8 May, acknowledged but not viewed as of 29 June per Next Step), signaling low priority, low urgency, or a mismatch between the content and their needs. No live discovery call has occurred to validate fit, create engagement, or build urgency. Passive video consumption is not moving the deal forward.

No competitive intelligence: unknown whether Estremar is sole-sourcing Zycus or evaluating multiple P2P and SIM vendors (Coupa, SAP Ariba, GEP, Ivalua, Jaggaer), and unknown whether they have an incumbent being replaced. Cannot position differentiation (AI/Merlin negotiation, Agentic AI, LATAM compliance) or counter a rival without this context.

Single-threaded at analyst level with no economic buyer or decision maker engagement. CFO approved pricing second-hand 64 days ago but has never engaged directly. No executive sponsor. Cannot progress past Qualified without CFO or VP Finance engagement.

Close date 17 September (82 days out) is not credible for a Qualified-stage deal with no live buyer engagement in 30 days, no EB, no champion, no decision process visibility, and no commercial conversation. Deal has been in Qualified 64 days with no progression. Close date already slipped 67 days on 30 May.

Buyer has not viewed demo videos 51 days after delivery despite two commitments (8 May sent, 28 May said would view second week June, 29 June still not viewed). Signals low priority, low urgency, or mismatch between async video content and buyer's actual need. No live discovery call to create engagement.
No competitive intelligence. Unknown if Estremar is sole-sourcing or evaluating other P2P/supplier portal vendors. Cannot position differentiation or counter a rival without this context.

No quantified business case. Pain articulated (manual AP workload prevents analysis), but no cost, cycle time, error rate, or ROI figure. Cannot build a defensible value case or CFO-ready business justification without metrics.

champion_strength.source: OpportunityContactRole, Task log, Next_Step 8 May to 29 June
champion_strength.summary: Weak. Gianluca Pansera is the only identified contact, an Analyst of Accounts for Pay who articulated requirements and said the CFO approved pricing, but who has not viewed demo videos 51 days after delivery and has not facilitated any upward or lateral stakeholder engagement. He is a friendly evaluator, not a champion. No access to economic buyer, no coaching on how to win, no advocacy. Single-threaded at the wrong level.

items.action: Book a live 30-minute discovery call with Pansera to validate supplier portal fit and surface objections.
items.trigger: Demo videos sent 8 May remain unviewed 51 days later despite two commitments (28 May, 29 June). Async video content is not creating engagement or urgency. Pansera is responsive in email but passive on follow-through.
items.expected_effect: Convert passive stall to active qualification. A live call will validate the 5-point supplier portal requirement, surface any fit gaps or objections Pansera has not stated, and reveal if he is the true driver or gathering info for someone else. Will also test engagement level and set next milestone (CFO intro or technical deep-dive).

items.action: Request a 20-minute intro call with CFO to confirm budget, timeline, and approval chain.
items.trigger: Pansera stated 23 April that CFO Diego Pace approved $80k-$120k pricing, but CFO has no contact role, no logged engagement, and approval is second-hand and 64 days old. Cannot progress past Qualified without economic buyer identification and engagement.
items.expected_effect: Break single-threading and map budget authority. CFO call will confirm pricing approval is still valid, surface decision timeline and approval chain, and create executive-level sponsorship for a $100k deal at a company preparing for IPO. Will move deal from Qualified to the relevant stakeholder if CFO confirms active evaluation.

items.action: Qualify competitive landscape and urgency tied to IPO preparation timeline.
items.trigger: No competitor named in any source. Unknown if sole-source or multi-vendor eval. IPO preparation mentioned as compelling event 31 March but no timeline or deadline stated. Cannot position differentiation or counter a rival without competitive intel, and cannot forecast credibly without urgency anchor.
items.expected_effect: Surface true urgency and competitive positioning. On live call (move 1 or 2), ask directly: are you evaluating other platforms (Coupa, Ariba, Ivalua), what is driving the timeline, what happens if this is not done by Q4 2026. Will reveal if buyer has budget allocated to Zycus or is shopping, and whether close date is real or placeholder.

items.action: Position Merlin analytics and ANA as the intelligence layer for IPO readiness and CFO reporting.
items.trigger: AIS 3.0 Negative. Email thread and demo videos are tactical P2P/SIM with no AI narrative. Pansera's pain (cannot do analysis, no KPIs) and compelling event (IPO prep, capital markets scrutiny, new CFO mandate for reporting) create a wedge for Merlin spend analytics, contract risk, supplier performance, and ANA predictive cash flow as the differentiated intelligence layer. Wedge is unactivated.
items.expected_effect: Elevate conversation from workflow automation to strategic decision support. Merlin and ANA position Zycus as the platform that not only automates AP but also gives the CFO and new directors the spend visibility, risk signals, and predictive analytics capital markets will scrutinize. Differentiates from pure P2P competitors and aligns with IPO readiness mandate.

items.action: Build a quantified business case with AP cycle time, cost per invoice, and annual savings estimate.
items.trigger: Pain articulated (manual AP workload prevents analysis) but no cost, cycle time, error rate, or ROI quantified. Cannot build CFO-ready justification or defend $100k investment without metrics. Pansera owns the pain; he can provide the baseline data.
items.expected_effect: Create a defensible ROI case for CFO approval. Quantified savings (e.g. 40% cycle time reduction, $50k annual AP cost savings, 3 hours/day freed for analysis) will anchor the business case and give Pansera a CFO-ready narrative to drive internal approval. Will also surface if budget is real or soft.

confidence_signals.summary: Deal is cooling. Last buyer engagement 30 days ago (28 May). Demo videos sent 51 days ago remain unviewed despite two buyer commitments. Single contact at analyst level, no upward engagement, no live call ever held. Close date slipped 67 days on 30 May. No stage progression in 64 days. Rep outreach 29 June notes videos still not viewed. Engagement is passive and stalled.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 24 April 2026, qualified same day. In Qualified stage 64 days with no progression. Close date slipped 67 days (from 12 July to 17 September) on 30 May. Now 82 days to close. Last buyer engagement 28 May (30 days ago), when Pansera said he would view demo videos in second week of June; that week passed 2-3 weeks ago with no follow-up. Rep reached out 29 June noting videos still not viewed. Single action stalled 50 days (demo videos sent 8 May, acknowledged but never reviewed). Time-in-Qualified (64 days) is excessive for a $100k deal with 82 days remaining and no commercial, EB, or decision process visibility.
evidence: Demo videos sent 8 May 2026, acknowledged by Pansera but not viewed as of 29 June (51 days later) despite his 28 May commitment to review in second week of June (source: Next_Step__c, Task 28 May)

Single contact role at Analyst level (Gianluca Pansera, Analyst of Accounts for Pay); no economic buyer, decision maker, or executive sponsor identified (source: OpportunityContactRole, SF MEDDPICC flags)
CFO budget approval mentioned 23 April 2026 in email thread, but CFO has no contact role on opportunity and has never joined a call (source: Task email 23 April)
Close date slipped 67 days from 12 July to 17 September on 30 May 2026, the same day Pansera said he would view videos next week (source: OpportunityFieldHistory)
Last buyer engagement 28 May 2026 (30 days ago); no live discovery or qualification call ever held (both Avoma meetings were cancelled or not recorded) (source: LastActivityDate, Avoma manifest)

north_star_verdict.headline: Single-threaded analyst contact ghosted 30 days; demo videos ignored 51 days despite June commitment. No EB, no discovery call ever held, 64 days stuck in Qualified with zero commercial progress toward September close.

flags: Multi-thread immediately: the deal is single-threaded at analyst level (Gianluca Pansera, Evaluator) with no contact roles for the economic buyer, decision maker, or executive sponsor. For a $100k P2P and SIM procurement, budget authority sits with the CFO or VP Finance (account contact the relevant stakeholder, CFO, is not on the opp contact roles and has not engaged). Ask Pansera for an introduction to the finance or procurement leader who will approve the commercial decision, and add them as a contact role to expand influence and secure active sponsorship before the deal stalls further.

Deal has gone quiet with no buyer activity in 29 days (last touch 28 May) and no forward commitment from the 15 June rep outreach (buyer acknowledged but took no action). This is a low-priority signal. Re-engage within the next 7 days with a provocative insight (e.g., a LATAM peer-reference story, a Merlin agentic AI ROI case, or a procurement-benchmark data point) that creates urgency, and ask Pansera for a go/no-go decision: if Estremar is not ready to evaluate now, disqualify and return in Q4 2026 when their priority may shift. Do not continue passive follow-ups with a disengaged contact.

Map the competitive landscape and procurement process before the next conversation: it is unknown whether Estremar is sole-sourcing Zycus or evaluating multiple P2P and SIM vendors (Coupa, SAP Ariba, GEP, Ivalua), whether an incumbent exists, and what their decision process and timeline are (RFI, RFP, vendor shortlist, POC). Without this context, Zycus cannot position differentiation (AI/Merlin negotiation, Agentic AI, LATAM compliance) or counter a competitor. On the next call, ask Pansera directly: How are you addressing P2P and supplier management today? Are you evaluating other vendors alongside Zycus? Who are they, and what are your top criteria for choosing? Walk me through your procurement process and timeline from now through contract signature. This surfaces the competitive landscape and the path to close so the deal can be shaped to their real process and decision criteria.
best_practice_check.summary: Single-threaded at analyst level with no economic buyer engagement 82 days from close; demo engagement stalled 51 days; no live discovery call to validate fit or create urgency.

competitive_position.summary: No competitive intelligence exists. Salesforce Competitors__c is blank, no synthesis-map fields name a rival or incumbent, and the unrecorded intro call (23 March) provides no context. It is unknown whether Estremar is evaluating other P2P and SIM vendors (Coupa, SAP Ariba, GEP, Ivalua, Jaggaer, Proactis, or others), whether they have an incumbent system being replaced, or whether this is a sole-source opportunity. The lack of competitive data is a major blind spot: Zycus cannot position its Merlin agentic AI differentiation, cannot counter a rival's narrative, and cannot tailor the commercial strategy. The move is to surface the competitive landscape in the next discovery call by asking Pansera who else is being evaluated and what their shortlist looks like.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Under-positioned. AIS 3.0 Negative (AI Resistant). Products__c includes Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, and ANA, but the email thread and Next_Step show only P2P and SIM (supplier portal, invoice automation) framing with no Merlin, agentic, or AI narrative. Pansera's requirements are tactical workflow automation (supplier portal, 3-way matching, ERP sync), not strategic AI-led transformation. The demo videos sent 8 May covered supplier portal and PO/invoice mechanics but no mention of Merlin negotiation agent, ANA, or agentic intake. The AI story has not been told, and the buyer has shown no AI appetite. Given the IPO preparation context and the new CFO's mandate for reporting and controls, there is a wedge to position Merlin and ANA as the differentiated intelligence layer (spend analytics, contract risk, supplier performance, predictive cash flow) that elevates Estremar from process automation to decision support ahead of capital markets scrutiny. That wedge is unactivated.

items.quote: Pansera 24 April: las ordenes de compra viajen del ERP al portal, proveedor carga factura, flujo de autorización, factura viaje al ERP e impacte (asiento contable, cuenta corriente, libro IVA), comunicación en portal, aviso de pago. Bailey 24 April: Podemos apoyar completamente el flujo de órdenes de compra y la automatización de facturas.
items.criterion: Supplier portal: PO routing from ERP to supplier, invoice upload by supplier, approval workflow, ERP sync with automated accounting entries, payment notifications

items.quote: Pansera 30 April: si, hay necesidad de 3-way matching, ya que la orden de compra, el remito y la factura tienen que coincidir. Proceso de aprobación en ERP, recepcionar orden antes de cargar factura.
items.criterion: 3-way matching (PO, receipt, invoice) with ERP-based PO approval and goods receipt workflow

items.quote: Pansera 31 March: necesidad no menor, es que una vez que la factura viaje del Portal al ERP, se genere el asiento contable correspondiente. Facturas impactan en más módulos, no solo asientos contables. No technical scoping or integration deep-dive has occurred.
items.criterion: ERP integration: invoices must generate accounting entries (journal, AP ledger, VAT book) automatically on sync

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Partial fit visibility. Estremar's stated requirements (supplier portal with PO routing, invoice upload, approval workflow, ERP sync, 3-way matching, automated accounting entries) align with Zycus iSupplier and eInvoicing capabilities. Bailey confirmed Zycus can support the full flow on 24 April. However, fit assessment is based on one analyst's email description, not a live demo, use-case workshop, or technical deep-dive. No BRD review, no shoe-fit validation, no multi-stakeholder confirmation. Fit appears aligned at the functional level but unvalidated at the decision-committee level.

hard.ais_why:
• Both Estremar meetings were either cancelled or had no recording, so there is no spoken AI signal to anchor on.
• SF Next_Step shows only 'demo videos sent' for a P2P+SIM scope with no Merlin, ANA, intake or agentic mentions in the opp description.
• Sparse data, no transcripts, and a tactical P2P/SIM frame point to Resistant by default.

deltas.subject: No competitive intelligence. Unknown if Estremar is sole-sourcing or evaluating other P2P/supplier portal vendors. Cannot position differentiation or counter a rival without this context.

deltas.to: No quantified business case. Pain articulated (manual AP workload prevents analysis), but no cost, cycle time, error rate, or ROI figure. Cannot build a defensi
deltas.subject: No quantified business case. Pain articulated (manual AP workload prevents analysis), but no cost, cycle time, error rate, or ROI figure. Cannot build a defensible value case or CFO-ready business justification without metrics.

deltas.to: Break single-threading: escalate to CFO or VP Finance to map budget authority, approval chain, and decision timeline. Pansera mentioned CFO approved pricing on
deltas.subject: Break single-threading: escalate to CFO or VP Finance to map budget authority, approval chain, and decision timeline. Pansera mentioned CFO approved pricing on 23 April but CFO has never engaged directly; his approval is second-hand and 64 days old. A $100k SaaS deal at a company preparing for IPO requires CFO or finance VP sponsorship. Use Pansera's CFO reference to request a 20-minute intro call with the relevant stakeholder to confirm fit, timeline, and next steps.

deltas.to: Convert passive video review to live engagement: Pansera has not viewed demo videos 51 days after delivery despite two commitments. Videos alone do not create u
deltas.subject: Convert passive video review to live engagement: Pansera has not viewed demo videos 51 days after delivery despite two commitments. Videos alone do not create urgency or qualify fit. Propose a live 30-minute discovery call (in Spanish if needed) to walk through the supplier portal flow, validate the 5-point requirement set, and surface any gaps or objections. A live call will also reveal if Pansera is the true driver or if he is gathering info for someone else.

deltas.to: Qualify competitive landscape and urgency: unknown if Estremar is sole-sourcing or evaluating multiple vendors, and no timeline anchored to the IPO preparation
deltas.subject: Qualify competitive landscape and urgency: unknown if Estremar is sole-sourcing or evaluating multiple vendors, and no timeline anchored to the IPO preparation compelling event. On the live call, ask directly: are you evaluating other P2P or supplier portal platforms, what is driving the timeline, and what happens if this is not done by Q4 2026. This will surface competition and true urgency (or lack thereof).

deltas.subject: Gianluca Pansera

deltas.to: Multi-thread immediately: the deal is single-threaded at analyst level (Gianluca Pansera, Evaluator) with no contact roles for the economic buyer, decision make
deltas.subject: Multi-thread immediately: the deal is single-threaded at analyst level (Gianluca Pansera, Evaluator) with no contact roles for the economic buyer, decision maker, or executive sponsor. For a $100k P2P and SIM procurement, budget authority sits with the CFO or VP Finance (account contact the relevant stakeholder, CFO, is not on the opp contact roles and has not engaged). Ask Pansera for an introduction to the finance or procurement leader who will approve the commercial decision, and add them as a contact role to expand influence and secure active sponsorship before the deal stalls further.

deltas.to: Run a live discovery call to re-engage and validate fit: demo videos sent 8 May have not been viewed as of 29 June (48 days later), and the buyer has not respon
deltas.subject: Run a live discovery call to re-engage and validate fit: demo videos sent 8 May have not been viewed as of 29 June (48 days later), and the buyer has not responded to rep outreach since 30 May (27 days ago). Passive video consumption is not creating engagement or urgency. Propose a 30-minute live discovery call with Pansera (and ideally the finance or procurement lead) to surface the specific operational pain (invoice errors, spend visibility gaps, supplier risk, manual bottlenecks), quantify the impact, and tailor a demo to their top 3 decision criteria. This shifts from one-way content delivery to two-way value creation and tests whether the buyer is genuinely engaged or deprioritizing Zycus.

deltas.to: Map the competitive landscape and procurement process before the next conversation: it is unknown whether Estremar is sole-sourcing Zycus or evaluating multiple
deltas.subject: Map the competitive landscape and procurement process before the next conversation: it is unknown whether Estremar is sole-sourcing Zycus or evaluating multiple P2P and SIM vendors (Coupa, SAP Ariba, GEP, Ivalua), whether an incumbent exists, and what their decision process and timeline are (RFI, RFP, vendor shortlist, POC). Without this context, Zycus cannot position differentiation (AI/Merlin negotiation, Agentic AI, LATAM compliance) or counter a competitor. On the next call, ask Pansera directly: How are you addressing P2P and supplier management today? Are you evaluating other vendors alongside Zycus? Who are they, and what are your top criteria for choosing? Walk me through your procurement process and timeline from now through contract signature. This surfaces the competitive landscape and the path to close so the deal can be shaped to their real process and decision criteria.

deltas.to: Single-threaded at analyst level (Pansera, Evaluator role) with no economic buyer or business champion engaged; for a $100k enterprise SaaS deal, CFO or VP Fina
deltas.subject: Single-threaded at analyst level (Pansera, Evaluator role) with no economic buyer or business champion engaged; for a $100k enterprise SaaS deal, CFO or VP Finance sign-off is required, and the absence of exec sponsorship 63 days into the cycle is a blocker. Add buyer-side contact roles to the opportunity (data-hygiene gap: only 1 buyer role on record) and multi-thread upward to the CFO, procurement lead, or operations director who owns the P2P and SIM pain and controls the budget. Ask Pansera for an introduction to the budget authority within the next 7 days.

deltas.to: Demo-video strategy has not created engagement: videos sent 8 May, buyer acknowledged 30 May and 15 June but has not viewed them (48 days post-delivery). This s
deltas.subject: Demo-video strategy has not created engagement: videos sent 8 May, buyer acknowledged 30 May and 15 June but has not viewed them (48 days post-delivery). This signals a mismatch between the content and the buyer's priorities, or low intent. Pivot from passive video delivery to a live discovery call that surfaces Estremar's specific pain (e.g., manual procurement cycles, invoice errors, supplier onboarding lag), quantifies it (cost, time, or risk), and maps the decision criteria and process. Use the call to assess whether Pansera can be developed into a champion or whether Zycus needs a different stakeholder.

deltas.to: Deal has gone quiet with no buyer activity in 29 days (last touch 28 May) and no forward commitment from the 15 June rep outreach (buyer acknowledged but took n
deltas.subject: Deal has gone quiet with no buyer activity in 29 days (last touch 28 May) and no forward commitment from the 15 June rep outreach (buyer acknowledged but took no action). This is a low-priority signal. Re-engage within the next 7 days with a provocative insight (e.g., a LATAM peer-reference story, a Merlin agentic AI ROI case, or a procurement-benchmark data point) that creates urgency, and ask Pansera for a go/no-go decision: if Estremar is not ready to evaluate now, disqualify and return in Q4 2026 when their priority may shift. Do not continue passive follow-ups with a disengaged contact.

value.detail: Close date 17 September (83 days out) is not credible for a Qualified-stage deal with no live buyer engagement in 29 days, no champion, no pain articulation, no decision criteria, no commercial discussions, and no forward milestone booked. The deal has been in Qualified stage for 63 days with no progression. To close 17 September requires moving through Formal Evaluation, Shortlisted, Vendor Selected, Negotiation, and Contract stages in 83 days (average 16 days per stage), yet the deal has shown zero velocity and the buyer has deprioritized (demo videos unviewed 48 days after delivery).

packets.subject: No quantified business case. Pain articulated (manual AP workload prevents analysis), but no cost, cycle time, error rate, or ROI figure. Cannot build a defensible value case or CFO-ready business justification without metrics.

value.flag: Break single-threading: escalate to CFO or VP Finance to map budget authority, approval chain, and decision timeline. Pansera mentioned CFO approved pricing on 23 April but CFO has never engaged directly; his approval is second-hand and 64 days old. A $100k SaaS deal at a company preparing for IPO requires CFO or finance VP sponsorship. Use Pansera's CFO reference to request a 20-minute intro call with the relevant stakeholder to confirm fit, timeline, and next steps.

value.flag: Convert passive video review to live engagement: Pansera has not viewed demo videos 51 days after delivery despite two commitments. Videos alone do not create urgency or qualify fit. Propose a live 30-minute discovery call (in Spanish if needed) to walk through the supplier portal flow, validate the 5-point requirement set, and surface any gaps or objections. A live call will also reveal if Pansera is the true driver or if he is gathering info for someone else.

value.flag: Qualify competitive landscape and urgency: unknown if Estremar is sole-sourcing or evaluating multiple vendors, and no timeline anchored to the IPO preparation compelling event. On the live call, ask directly: are you evaluating other P2P or supplier portal platforms, what is driving the timeline, and what happens if this is not done by Q4 2026. This will surface competition and true urgency (or lack thereof).
